title: Barringtonia semisuta
intro: Barringtonia semisuta, also known as the small sea poison tree or sea poison tree, is a species of plant in the family Lecythidaceae, native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Barringtonia semisuta include alkaloids, flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Barringtonia semisuta is often prepared as a decoction or infused in water to make a tea, or used as a poultice to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Barringtonia semisuta is classified under the family Lecythidaceae and belongs to the genus Barringtonia. It is a species of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. The species is further categorized as a tree or shrub, with its exact classification varying across different sources. Barringtonia semisuta is often referred to as a member of the Lecythidaceae family, which is a part of the order Ericales. The plant's classification is primarily based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships.
